one_time_description: Subsequent event: Grant Agreement with Nature United for $3,000,000 related to Special Forest Management Agreement with Interfor and BC; $2,500,000 paid to Interfor for reduction in allowable annual cut of TFL45, $500,000 for transactional costs (occurred April 2024, after fiscal year end)
economic_notes: Nation holds 12.99% interest in A-Tlegay Fisheries Limited Partnership which acquires and manages fishing licences and quota. Saaiyouck Development Corporation (100% owned) investigates and pursues business opportunities in traditional territories. Related party 0986442 BC Ltd. trades in cryptocurrency. Guardian Forest Keeper Barge under renovation ($144,020 under construction). Special Forest Management Area established within TFL45 and Great Bear Rainforest post year-end. Nation rents townhouse to related family member of Chief and Council.
notes: Comparative 2023 figures are restated (Note 14): A-Tlegay Fisheries LP corrected grant revenue netting error increasing Investment in GBP by $412,687 and accumulated surplus by $412,687; also $15,000 CIRNAC loan reclassified from deferred revenue to long-term debt. No schedule of remuneration provided. Honoraria of $190,870 paid (Chief/Council/others) but individual amounts not disclosed. Expenses_enterprises represents Economic Development segment. OSR calculated as total revenue minus ISC ($626,756) and provincial ($276,000) and Kwakiutl District Council ($5,574) transfers. Consolidated entities include Matsayno Sustainability Corporation and Matsayno Sustainability Limited Partnership. GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: A-Tlegay Fisheries LP (12.99%) and Saaiyouck Development Corporation (100%). Subsequent event: $3M Nature United grant and Special Forest Management Agreement with Interfor executed April 2024, with $2.5M already paid to Interfor.
